title: Asiatic Lions Conservation and Barda Wildlife Sanctuary
focus: Conservation initiatives for Asiatic Lions, specifically Project Lion and the emergence of Barda Wildlife Sanctuary as a secondary habitat.
rationale: Covers Project Lion and the geographical and ecological details of Barda WLS, which are highly testable in UPSC prelims for mapping and species conservation.
sectionExcerpt: Project Lion was announced in 2020 to expand lion habitats beyond Gir to areas like Girnar, Pania, Mitiyala, and Barda Wildlife Sanctuary (Gujarat). Under the WPA 2022 amendment, CITES-listed species are now under Schedule IV. Barda's key flora includes Gorad, Babul, and Bamboo, and fauna includes Sambar, chital, and chinkara.
evidenceBullets: Project Lion (2020) aims to expand habitats beyond Gir to Girnar, Pania, Mitiyala, and Barda.
Barda Wildlife Sanctuary (Porbandar and Devbhumi Dwarka, Gujarat) is emerging as a second home for Asiatic Lions.
WPA 2022 amendment places CITES-listed species under Schedule IV of the Act.

title: UNESCO's World Network of Biosphere Reserves (WNBR) & Cold Desert BR
focus: The MAB programme, WNBR designation criteria, India's Biosphere Reserves, and specific ecological and cultural details of the Cold Desert BR.
rationale: Biosphere reserves and UNESCO MAB are recurring UPSC themes. The specific inclusion of Cold Desert BR and its associated protected areas, biome, and culture makes it a high-yield area.
sectionExcerpt: UNESCO designated India's Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve to the WNBR. WNBR is managed by the MAB Programme (1971). Designation is by the UNESCO Director-General via the MAB ICC using the Seville Strategy. India has 13 BRs in the WNBR. The Cold Desert BR in Himachal Pradesh covers Pin Valley NP, Chandratal, and Sarchu & Kibber WLS. It features a cold desert biome (rain-shadow zone), distinct Buddhist culture (Gompas, Chorten, Mani Walls), and fauna like the Snow Leopard and Himalayan Ibex.
evidenceBullets: WNBR (launched 1976) is managed by UNESCO's Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme (launched 1971).
Designations are made by the Director-General of UNESCO following MAB ICC decisions, using the Seville Strategy.
India has 13 Biosphere Reserves recognized under WNBR.
Cold Desert BR (Himachal Pradesh) covers Pin Valley NP, Chandratal, and Sarchu & Kibber WLS.
Cold Desert BR lies in a rain-shadow zone with distinct Buddhist culture (Gompas, Chorten) and fauna like Snow Leopard and Himalayan Ibex.

title: Wildlife Protection Act (WPA), 1972 and 2022 Amendments
focus: The structural changes to WPA schedules, institutional bodies, and rules regarding hunting exceptions and translocation.
rationale: The WPA is a foundational environmental law for UPSC. The 2022 amendments reducing schedules to 4 and specific rules on hunting exceptions (Sections 11/12) are critical for prelims.
sectionExcerpt: Kerala introduced amendments to ease procedures for killing wild animals threatening humans. The WPA 1972 (2022 Amendment) features 4 Schedules: Schedule I (highest protection), II (lesser protection), III (protected plants), and IV (CITES species). It establishes bodies like the National Board for Wildlife (chaired by PM) and WCCB. Sections 11 and 12 allow the Chief Wild Life Warden to permit hunting of Schedule I and II animals in specific cases (e.g., threat to human life, disease). Translocation requires Central Government permission for Schedule I animals.
evidenceBullets: WPA 2022 Amendment reduced schedules to 4: Sch I (highest protection), Sch II (lesser protection), Sch III (plants), Sch IV (CITES species).
Establishes the National Board for Wildlife, chaired by the Prime Minister.
Chief Wild Life Warden can permit hunting of Schedule I and II animals under Sections 11 and 12 if they are dangerous to human life or diseased beyond recovery.
Translocation of Schedule I animals requires prior permission from the Central Government.

title: Biological Diversity (Access and Benefit Sharing) Regulation 2025
focus: Issuance of new ABS regulations by the National Biodiversity Authority.
rationale: Although brief, it introduces a newly updated regulatory framework by a statutory body (NBA) which is relevant for environmental governance tracking.
sectionExcerpt: The National Biodiversity Authority (NBA) has issued the new Biological Diversity (Access and Benefit Sharing) Regulation 2025.
evidenceBullets: The National Biodiversity Authority (NBA) issued the Biological Diversity (Access and Benefit Sharing) Regulation 2025.
